name: Guardo and Velilla del Río Carrión from Puente Almuhey – circular via Siero de la Reina
name_original: Guardo y Velilla del Río Carrión desde Puente Almuhey – circular por Siero de la Reina
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Valderrueda traverse the Eastern Leonese Mountains, characterized by significant elevation and numerous mountain passes. The region features valleys shaped by the Tuéjar and Céa rivers, alongside extensive oak forests. This mountainous terrain provides challenging ascents and descents, with an average altitude of 1,030 meters. The landscape offers diverse routes suitable for road cyclists seeking varied conditions. Best road cycling routes around Valderrueda The most popular road cycling route is Guardo and Velilla del Río Carrión from Puente Almuhey – circular via Siero de la Reina, a 41.8 miles (67.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Saldaña from Guardo – circular via Resp, a moderate 57.8 miles (93.1 km) path. This route offers rolling hills and scenic views through the region's varied landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Respenda de la Peña from Guardo, a 74.4 miles (119.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and river valleys, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Valderrueda is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Valderrueda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valderrueda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Valderrueda?
A: Valderrueda, nestled in the Eastern Leonese Mountains, is characterized by significant elevation and numerous mountain passes. You'll find challenging ascents and descents, with an average altitude of 1,030 meters. The landscape features valleys shaped by the Tuéjar and Céa rivers, alongside extensive oak forests, offering diverse routes from moderate to very difficult.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Valderrueda?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Valderrueda, catering to various skill levels. Approximately half of these are classified as moderate, while the other half are difficult, ensuring options for both experienced cyclists and those looking for a significant challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Valderrueda area?
A: Yes, Valderrueda offers several circular road cycling routes. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042296134/guardo-and-velilla-del-rio-carrion-from-puente-almuhey-circular-via-siero-de-la-reina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo and Velilla del Río Carrión from Puente Almuhey – circular via Siero de la Reina&lt;/a&gt; covers 67.3 km with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154588183/saldana-from-guardo-circular-via-resp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldaña from Guardo – circular via Resp&lt;/a&gt;, which is longer at 93.1 km but features rolling hills.

Q: What are some notable mountain passes for road cyclists in Valderrueda?
A: The Valderrueda region is famous for its challenging mountain passes. Cyclists can tackle renowned passes like San Glorio, Pandetrave, Pando, Monteviejo, and San Isidro. These passes are considered 'natural fortresses of challenging mountains' and offer demanding climbs and exhilarating descents. You can also explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Picones (1316 m)&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4948766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pando Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of views and landmarks can I expect along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Valderrueda offer spectacular panoramic views, especially from higher points like the Alto de Valderrueda. You can also incorporate the picturesque Riaño Reservoir into your rides, accessible via passes like Pando or Monteviejo. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Carande and Las Pintas Massif&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcayo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for advanced cyclists?
A: Yes, advanced cyclists will find several long and challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panderrueda Pass – View of the Picos de Europa loop from Guardo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 144.4 km route with over 2,200 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170708853/respenda-de-la-pena-from-guardo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Respenda de la Peña from Guardo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 120 km with significant climbing.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Valderrueda?
A: The road cycling routes around Valderrueda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. Over 1,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging ascents and the stunning natural beauty of the Eastern Leonese Mountains.

Q: Are there any historical routes suitable for road cycling in Valderrueda?
A: Yes, the region features historical routes that add a unique dimension to your ride. You can explore segments of the Roman Road of El Pando and the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental, which are part of the extensive road network in Valderrueda.

Q: What natural attractions are near the road cycling routes in Valderrueda?
A: Beyond the cycling routes, Valderrueda is close to significant natural attractions. The Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park offers rich biodiversity and stunning backdrops. The Picos de Europa National Park is also only 5.5 km away, providing additional opportunities for exploration. You can also visit natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/306845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilbo Peak&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6811232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted Pine Forest with Wooden Masks&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Valderrueda?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for challenging conditions, the best time for road cycling in Valderrueda is generally during the warmer months, from late spring through early autumn. This period offers more stable weather, clearer passes, and pleasant temperatures for tackling the region's significant elevations.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes for those seeking less extreme challenges?
A: Absolutely. While Valderrueda is known for its difficult climbs, there are also moderate routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velilla del Río Carrión – Puerto de Picones (1316 m) loop from Guardo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 73.25 km route with over 880 meters of elevation gain, offering a good balance of challenge and scenic beauty.
tips: Guardo is a municipality in the province of Palencia, Castilla y León, on the banks of the Carrión river. The town is the gateway to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park which attracts many adventure tourists to the area.

Velilla del Río Carrión sits within a spectacular landscape in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, spectacular landscapes. As its name indicates, the town sits on the banks of the Carrión River. A great place to start any adventure and you might even see a grizzly bear.
Mountain pass at 1275 masl on the route between the Montaña Palentina Natural Park and the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park.
Beautiful Puerto Leones, with little slope and very continuous. The northern slope has rougher asphalt and is more shady. The southern slope has good asphalt and is very sunny.
Morgovejo is a small town in the northeast of the province of León with origins in the 9th century, the town is located on the commercial route of the Cea River.
